title: CPS Header Schema
description: JSON Schema for validating YAML frontmatter headers in Cardano Problem Statements (CPSs). Most field-level value rules are enforced by the validation script with friendly messages; the schema enforces structural/type constraints.

CPS.description: The CPS number (positive integer, without leading 0), or a placeholder before being assigned: a single quoted "?", 'unassigned', or 'pending'. Value-level checks (positive, no leading zero) are enforced by the script.

Title.description: A succinct and descriptive title. Don't use backticks (`) in titles since they disrupt formatting in other contexts. Length cap and backtick check enforced by the script.

Category.description: One of the editorially accepted categories covering one area of the ecosystem

Status.description: Status of the CPS: Open, Solved, or Inactive with an optional parenthetical reason. Format enforced by the script.

Authors.description: A list of authors' real names and email addresses (e.g. John Doe <john.doe@email.domain>). Per-entry format enforced by the script.

Proposed Solutions.description: A list of CIPs addressing the problem, if any. Can be an empty array []. Each entry is 'Label: URL'. When label is CIP-NNNN, URL must be a GitHub CIPs repo link; use '?' suffix for PRs (candidates).

Discussions.description: A list of links where major technical discussions regarding this CPS happened. Must include a link to the pull request that created the CPS. Each entry must have a label followed by a colon and URL. Can be a string 'Label: URL' or a dictionary {'Label': 'URL'}.

Created.description: Date created on, in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) format. Format enforced by the script.

License.description: Abbreviation of an approved license. Allowed value enforced by the script (with a did-you-mean hint on near-misses).
